elements.sent-2411-002: We present a distributed algorithm to track the fixed points of time-varying quadratic programs when agents can (i) sample their objective function asynchronously, (ii) compute new iterates asynchronously, and (iii) communicate asynchronously.
elements.sent-2411-003: We show that even for a time-varying strongly convex quadratic program, asynchronous sampling of objectives can cause agents to minimize a certain form of nonconvex ``aggregate'' objective function.
elements.sent-2411-004: Then, we show that by minimizing the aggregate objective, agents still track the solution of the original quadratic program to within an error ball dependent upon (i) agents' tracking error when solving the aggregate problem, and (ii) the time variation of the original quadratic objective.
elements.sent-2411-005: Lastly, we show that this work generalizes existing work, in the sense that synchronizing the agents' behaviors recovers existing convergence results (up to constants).
elements.sent-2411-006: Simulations show the robustness of our algorithm to asynchrony.
elements.sent-2411-007: Time-varying optimization problems arise in applications including robotics~\cite{arslan2019sensor , signal processing~\cite{jakubiec2012d , and machine learning~\cite{yang2016online ; see~\cite{simonetto2020time for survey.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: Such problems can model time-varying demands in power distribution systems~\cite{tang2017real and robot navigation in cluttered dynamic environments~\cite{arslan2016exact , among other problems~\cite{dall2016optimal,cortes2017coordinated .}}}
elements.sent-2411-009: It is common in time-varying optimization for agents to sample a continuously varying objective at discrete points in time due to the use of digital processing units~\cite{simonetto2016class , and it is often assumed that all agents sample the continuous-time problem at the same times.}
elements.sent-2411-010: However, it is well known that it can be difficult to synchronize agents' behaviors, including sampling, computations, and communications.
elements.sent-2411-011: Accordingly, in this work we develop a distributed gradient optimization algorithm in which agents can asynchronously compute, communicate, and sample their objective, subject only to bounded delays.
elements.sent-2411-012: We propose a block-based gradient optimization algorithm, and algorithms of this kind have been used for many time-invariant optimization problems~\cite{bertsekas1989parallel,yazdani2021asynchronous,cannelli2020asynchronous,tseng1991rate,luo1992error,ubl2022faster,ubl21,hendrickson23,hendrickson23b .}
elements.sent-2411-013: Recently, such algorithms have been applied to time-varying optimization problems as well~\cite{behrendt2023totally,behrendt2023distributed .}
elements.sent-2411-014: We focus on time-varying, strongly convex quadratic programs (QPs), which have been used across a range of applications~\cite{simonetto2020time .}
elements.sent-2411-015: However, we show that under asynchronous objective sampling, agents' collective behavior is in general equivalent to minimizing a time-varying, nonconvex quadratic objective function.
elements.sent-2411-016: We then show that agents track the solutions of such time-varying nonconvex problems to within an error ball whose size we bound; these results on asynchronous time-varying nonconvex optimization may be of independent interest.
elements.sent-2411-017: Then we show that agents track the solution of the original, time-varying QP to within an error ball that depends on (i) the number of computations and communications that agents execute between samples of the objective, and (ii) the rate of change of the underlying problem over time.
elements.sent-2411-018: To summarize, this paper makes the following contributions:.
elements.sent-2411-019: We show that asynchronous objective sampling leads, in general, to agents solving a time-varying nonconvex problem (Theorem~\ref{thm:agg ).}
elements.sent-2411-020: We present a distributed algorithm and prove that it tracks solutions of the aforementioned time-varying nonconvex problems to within a known error bound (Theorem~\ref{thm:1 ).}
elements.sent-2411-021: We show that agents track the solution of the original QP to within an error ball of known size~{(Theorem~\ref{thm:2 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-022: We show that by synchronizing agents' computations, communications, and objective sampling we recover (up to constant factors) existing linear convergence results for asynchronous algorithms (Corollaries~\ref{cor:synch1 and~\ref{cor:synch2 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-023: We demonstrate the ability of our algorithm to track the solutions of an asynchronously sampled time-varying optimization problem in two simulations~{(Section~\ref{sec:simulation ) .}}
elements.sent-2411-024: Distributed time-varying optimization has been studied for several classes of problems in continuous time~\cite{rahili2016distributed,wu2022distributed and discrete time~\cite{bernstein2018asynchronous,simonetto2017decentralized,ling2013decentralized,yi2020distributed .}}
elements.sent-2411-025: These discrete-time works have focused on agents that sample objective functions at each time, and we differ by considering asynchronous sampling.
elements.sent-2411-026: This was named in~\cite{simonetto2020time as an open problem, and to the best of our knowledge we are the first to address it.}
elements.sent-2411-027: This work also extends our previous work in~\cite{behrendt2023totally,behrendt2023distributed , which required synchronous sampling of objectives as well.}
